summary:
The muscles of mastication—the temporalis, masseter, and pterygoids—are mainly supplied by the second (pterygoid) part of the maxillary artery through branches such as the masseteric and deep temporal arteries. This distinguishes them from other parts of the maxillary artery, ensuring precise vascular support for jaw movement functions.  

Note: Only include information directly tied to the anatomical origin of musculature involved; avoid general statements about function unless clinically essential.
